source: Approximate nearest neighbor algorithm based on navigable (Information Systems).pdf

concepts.finding: Navigable Small World (NSW) graphs provide approximate k-NN search with logarithmic complexity by exploiting the small-world property: short average path length between any two nodes with long-range links.
concepts.implication: Small-world graph structures enable efficient approximate search in metric spaces, forming the foundation for modern ANN search algorithms like HNSW.
concepts.open_question: How does NSW graph construction cost scale with dimensionality and dataset size for search embedding spaces?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: NSW graph-based retrieval will achieve 90% recall with O(log n) search complexity on query embedding datasets up to 10M points.
